Private Credit Impulse Signal
← Back to dashboard

Private Credit Impulse Signal

Private credit impulse: momentum in bank and consumer credit growth.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Private Credit Impulse (PCI) of 1.78 and a standardized business loan Z-score of 1.78, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not Mean reversion (1).
  • The signal has transitioned into an Accelerating regime, marking a sharp departure from the previous Stable trend (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: High.

Methodology Applied:

  • Composite values above 0.75 indicate accelerating credit expansion and easing financing conditions (1).
  • A two-period confirmation rule is required to validate a regime shift and reduce whipsaws (1).
  • Rising values imply an expansionary bias and risk-on environment (1).
  • Private Credit Impulse Signal: latest observation 2026-03-31 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the surge in the 6-month annualized business loan growth rate (BUSLOANS_ann6) to 10.2% (1).
  • Positive momentum is evident as the PCI jumped from 0.66 to 1.78 in a single period (1).
  • Internal Tension: The latest print lacks consumer credit data (TOTALSL), creating an incomplete composite picture (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A reversal of the PCI below 0.60 would signal a failed transition (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Expansion continues as business demand for credit accelerates.
  • Upside secondary: Sustained credit boom triggered by broad-based easing across consumer and bank sectors.
  • Downside residual: Signal reversal triggered by high revision sensitivity or data gaps in consumer loans.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months); the signal identifies shifts in liquidity and financing conditions (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low; although the impulse is strong, the lack of two-period confirmation and missing sub-components necessitates caution.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs liquidity and demand dimensions by quantifying the velocity of aggregate credit creation (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Rising credit velocity implies rising demand and easing bank constraints, supporting risk-on sentiment (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with business sentiment surveys and real GDP growth expectations (1).

Regime Context:

  • The signal is newly entered into the Accelerating regime after 11 months of Stability (1).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening significantly, albeit without confirmation.

Model Limitations:

  • Frequent weekly revisions to H.8 bank data can distort early regime signals (1).
  • Nominal growth figures may be distorted during periods of high inflation (1).

Data & References:

  • Private Credit Impulse Signal (2026-03-31) (1).
  • Dominant datapoint: Z_BUSLOANS_ann6 at 1.78 (1).
  • Lending standards (SLOOS) and inflation (CPI/PCE) would improve reliability (1).

Private Credit Impulse Chart

Momentum in U.S. bank credit, business lending, and consumer loans.

All views expressed are personal, based on publicly available information, and do not represent the views of any employer or reflect any proprietary or internal analysis. This information should not be relied upon for making investment decisions.
